editors_note.headline: This week
editors_note.thesis: We're watching OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon — it's the one most likely to affect vendor and roadmap decisions this quarter.
editors_note.hook: We're flagging the lead for leadership — it's the one most likely to shift budgets or priorities.
editors_note.spotlight: The lead story is the most likely to change near-term decisions for teams shipping AI products.
editors_note.underhyped: Microsoft's Project Silica promises eternal storage. It can't get there from here deserves more attention than it's getting.
editors_note.risk: Keep an eye on Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government — it could shift budgets or vendor assumptions.

lead_story.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
lead_story.what_happened: OpenAI is making moves. Check whether your vendor assumptions still hold.
lead_story.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input (TechCrunch AI).
lead_story.what_to_do: Flag OpenAI for your leadership team — it may shift near-term priorities.

sections.title: Open-source picks
sections.deck: Open-source projects worth a closer look.

articles.title: CoPaw
articles.summary: CoPaw is a self-hosted AI assistant that runs locally or in the cloud, with integrations for DingTalk, Feishu, QQ, and Discord.
articles.what_happened: CoPaw ships a self-hosted AI assistant with multi-platform chat integrations (Discord, DingTalk, Feishu) and local or cloud deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: Self-hosted means your data stays on your infra — useful if you need an internal assistant without third-party API dependencies.
articles.what_to_do: Check CoPaw’s issue backlog and last commit date before depending on it for production use.
articles.our_take: The multi-platform angle is smart, but we’d want to see sustained maintainer activity before betting on it.

articles.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
articles.summary: IssueScout surfaces ‘good first issue’ labels across repos, enriched with community health scores and AI difficulty estimates for new contributors.
articles.what_happened: IssueScout launched as an open-source discovery platform that matches new contributors to welcoming repos using health scores and AI difficulty ratings.
articles.why_it_matters: New contributors waste hours on abandoned repos with mislabelled issues — IssueScout filters for active, well-maintained projects.
articles.what_to_do: Try IssueScout this week if you maintain an OSS project and want to attract first-time contributors.
articles.our_take: The AI difficulty estimate is the differentiator here — most ‘good first issue’ labels are unreliable without it.

sections.title: Infra
sections.deck: Cloud, scale, and infrastructure that matters to AI builders.

articles.title: Microsoft's Project Silica promises eternal storage. It can't get there from here
articles.summary: Project Silica uses femtosecond lasers to write 2 TB across hundreds of layers in glass readable for 10,000 years, but 20 Mbps write speeds make commercialisation unlikely.
articles.what_happened: Microsoft's Project Silica can store 2 TB in a 2 mm glass plate using femtosecond laser pulses, readable for 10,000 years.
articles.why_it_matters: At 20 Mbps write speed and no clear market, this is a research demo — not a storage product you'll buy anytime soon.
articles.what_to_do: Skip unless you're in archival storage. Check back in 2–3 years for write speed improvements.
articles.our_take: Cool physics, bad economics. We'd bet on DNA storage over glass plates for long-term archival.

articles.title: Firefox 149 beta develops a split personality
articles.summary: Firefox 149 beta promotes split-view mode to default-on, adds hardware-accelerated PDF rendering and expanded address autofill.
articles.what_happened: Firefox 149 beta (due March 24) enables split-view by default — two pages side-by-side in one window with a draggable divider.
articles.why_it_matters: Hardware-accelerated PDF rendering is the real story — could finally make Firefox usable for doc-heavy workflows.
articles.what_to_do: Test Firefox 149 beta if your app relies on PDF rendering or multi-panel layouts.
articles.our_take: Split-view is a nice-to-have. The PDF acceleration is what matters for web app developers.

articles.title: Iran all but vanishes from the global internet amid US-Israel strikes
articles.summary: Iran's internet connectivity dropped to near-zero during US-Israel military strikes, disrupting cloud services and CDN routing through the region.
articles.what_happened: Iran effectively disappeared from the global internet during US-Israel strikes, with BGP routes and DNS resolution failing across the country.
articles.why_it_matters: If you serve users in the Middle East or route traffic through Iranian peering, expect latency spikes and failover issues.
articles.what_to_do: Check your CDN and DNS failover paths for Middle East routing dependencies this week.
articles.our_take: This is an infra resilience story, not a geopolitics story. Test your failover, not your opinions.

sections.title: What changed in industry
sections.deck: Company moves, launches, and policy shifts that could affect your roadmap.
sections.promise: What changed and why it matters.

articles.title: Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government
articles.summary: The Trump administration is moving to ban Anthropic from US government contracts following the Pentagon dispute over Claude's military use.
articles.what_happened: Trump moved to ban Anthropic from federal contracts after the company refused to let Claude be used for Pentagon military applications.
articles.why_it_matters: If you're building on Claude for government clients, your procurement path just got harder — evaluate alternatives now.
articles.what_to_do: Audit your government-facing projects for Anthropic dependencies this week.
articles.our_take: Anthropic stood on principle, and it's costing them. Respect the move, but plan for the business reality.

articles.title: OpenAI and Amazon announce strategic partnership
articles.summary: The OpenAI announcement carries implications for budgets and vendor relationships (via OpenAI Blog).
articles.what_happened: New developments around OpenAI — relevant if it touches your stack or procurement.
articles.why_it_matters: The OpenAI move could shift the math for your next planning cycle; flag it in leadership review.
articles.what_to_do: Set a reminder to check OpenAI for downstream changes in 2 weeks.
articles.our_take: Competitive dynamics are shifting — pay attention to who benefits and who gets squeezed.

learn_this_week.title: Machine Learning Specialization
learn_this_week.note: Andrew Ng's refreshed ML curriculum. The gold standard intro. Audit free, cert paid.

prompt_of_the_week.text: Generate a week's worth of social media posts about [topic] in the voice of a technical thought leader — concise, opinionated, no fluff.
prompt_of_the_week.why: Technical audiences punish generic content. Specifying 'opinionated, no fluff' constrains the model away from safe, bland takes — the output reads more like a real practitioner's voice.
prompt_of_the_week.tip: Replace [topic] with your area. Add 2-3 example posts you admire for tone matching.
